meaning: n.马萨伊人(肯尼亚和坦桑尼亚的游牧狩猎民族)
examples: Lions attack a buffalo on the Masai Mara in Kenya.
Lions attack a buffalo in a swamp at the Masai Mara, Kenya.
For the Masai, there is nothing on earth more important than cattle.
The Masai Mara has previously been named the seventh new wonder of the world.
The Masai call this peak we're on, Africa's second-highest, "Erukenya" -- mountain of clouds.
